title: One-Pot Lemon Chicken Orzo Soup
description: This comforting and bright one-pot soup is packed with tender chicken, pasta, and a zesty lemon kick, making it a perfect family-friendly meal that's easy to clean up.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Cut chicken into 1-inch pieces. Season with salt and pepper. Add to the pot and cook until browned, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken and set aside.
Add chopped onion, carrots, and celery to the pot. Sauté until softened, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in chicken broth and bring to a boil. Add orzo pasta and cooked chicken. Reduce heat to medium-low, cover, and simmer for 10-12 minutes, or until orzo is tender.
Stir in fresh spinach until wilted, about 2-3 minutes.
Remove from heat. Squeeze in the juice of one lemon. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper as needed.
tips: For extra flavor, you can add a bay leaf to the broth while simmering and remove it before serving.
If you prefer a creamier soup, stir in a splash of heavy cream or a tablespoon of cream cheese at the end.
To make it even more kid-friendly, you can shred the chicken instead of dicing it, and ensure the vegetables are finely chopped.
